Get whole-body, women-focused care when you need it with Tia Essential, or join as a Tia member for a more personal, supported journey. Whichever path you choose, our commitment to your health and our high-quality care stays the same.
Tia is the only in-person and virtual clinic specifically designed for women across all stages of life

Unlike prescription-only or virtual-only options, we focus on your whole-body health. Unlike your traditional doctor, we’re highly specialized in women’s health. And unlike concierge medicine or other membership-based clinics, we offer a membership-free option. Here’s what you can expect from Tia:
✓ All-in-one care
All of your health needs, including primary care, gyn, labs, acupuncture, and more, all in one place
✓ Women-first, women-focused
High-quality care with highly specialized providers who deeply understand women’s bodies and your unique needs
✓ Fits your lifestyle
Get in-person care if you value that face-to-face, or fit virtual visits into your busy schedule
✓ Options for care
Tia Essential gets you quality care with no membership fee, or join as a Tia member for a more supported experience

children.text: The Tia Experience
What will your journey look like?

Everyone’s journey at Tia may look a little different. As a Tia Essential patient, you may come to us first for help with an acute issue like a UTI and then only see us 1x a year. And that’s okay! For Tia Essential patients and Tia members who want to dig a bit deeper into their health, we follow a three-step process that starts you on your
Whole Health Journey
, a holistic model designed to address your immediate concerns and long-term wellness.
Tia’s Whole Health Foundation
Tia’s Whole Health Foundation is built around three visits that brings your history, your labs, and your health concerns and goals into one unified Care Plan, truly personalized to you.
Healthcare is better when it’s connected

Most women manage their health through one-off, disconnected visits. At Tia, we take a Whole Health approach. Through a comprehensive questionnaire, we assess your wellbeing across nine core health factors, including nutrition, metabolism, family genetics, and more. This allows us to identify vulnerabilities early and build a personalized Care Plan tailored to your body, goals, and long-term health."
How it works
While every patient’s needs are unique and your specific visits may shift based on recent care or immediate concerns, this is the foundational path we take to ensure your care isn’t fragmented.
Step 1: New Patient Visit (Virtual Visit)

Meet with a provider virtually to review your medical history, discuss current concerns like prescription refills or symptoms, and begin building your personalized Care Plan. This visit ensures we understand your full health picture from the very start.
Step 2: Annual Preventative Exam (In-person Appointment)

Visit one of our clinics for a comprehensive preventative exam designed around women’s health. You may complete screenings such as blood work, a pelvic exam, pap test, or breast exam while your provider evaluates any additional labs or preventive screenings you may need.
Step 3: Labs & Care Plan Review (Virtual Visit)

Reconnect virtually to review your lab results together and refine your Care Plan based on new insights. We’ll outline clear next steps, long-term health goals, and any referrals needed to ensure your care stays proactive, connected, and personalized.
Care shouldn’t feel confusing or disconnected. Our step-by-step approach ensures you always know what’s next, with one provider who understands your full health story and keeps everything connected.
Not sure where to start?
"I have a specific concern."
Examples: You’re experiencing a new symptom, feeling unwell, or have a specific health problem you want to discuss.
→ RECOMMENDATION: New Patient Visit
"I’m due for a routine check-up"
Examples: You are looking for your annual exam, preventative screenings, or a general wellness check up.
→ RECOMMENDATION: Annual Exam
Better care leads to better health
On average, patients who begin with Tia's Whole Health Foundation rate their overall Tia experience a 9 out of 10.
Invest in your health today
Your health deserves more than quick fixes and disconnected visits. Begin with a personalized plan that brings your history, labs, and goals together so your care is always connected.

children.text: Getting started
A three-step process for getting started at Tia
Whether you choose Tia Essential or want to join as a Tia member, getting started is easy:
1. Check your insurance coverage
We’ll validate your insurance details when you sign up for Tia Essential or Tia and will confirm your in-network status before going forward.
2. Schedule your first appointment

Most patients begin with our new patient establishment of care visit to set the foundation for their health journey. During this time, we'll explore your current health concerns, goals, preferences, and health history.
3. Get started on your care journey

For those in need of an annual preventative exam, your provider may recommend this as a first step. If you have different needs, we'll collaborate with you to develop a care plan that works for you and your goals.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Feeling confused or judged about your experience with weight and metabolism is a common but frustrating experience. In fact, ~60% of patients report experiencing bias from doctors. When care focuses only on the scale or offers quick fixes, it often fails to look at the full picture of your physical, emotional, and metabolic health. With the rise of prescription solutions like GLP-1s, many women are also left wondering what is right for their body and how to navigate side effects or long-term management. This fragmented approach leaves you feeling stuck and like you've tried everything.

Tia’s Solution
Tia's Weight & Metabolic Health care is built to understand your whole body, not just a number. Our providers are trained on a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach , meaning the goal is helping
you
feel well, not fit someone else's idea of wellness. We create a personalized, non-judgmental Care Plan, integrating all factors from hormones and sleep to stress and nutrition for long-term support.

Experiencing unexplained weight gain or weight loss
Feeling stuck in current routines and like you’ve “tried everything” to manage your weight
Curious about or seeking GLP-1s, other weight management medications, or non-rx weight management solutions
Needing help managing chronic conditions like diabetes or high blood pressure.
Wanting a non-judgmental plan focused on long-term wellness and feeling well.
Seeking evaluation for associated issues like high cholesterol or sleep concerns.
Curious about metabolic health and midlife, and would like guidance building a proactive plan.

Why Weight & Metabolic Health matters for women
Metabolic Health is More Than a Number

Weight and metabolism are complex, often emotionally-charged subjects. Because metabolic health doesn't happen in a vacuum, our providers look at the bigger picture—from family history to hormones—to create a holistic path forward. We reject the idea that issues are caused by poor self-image or self-control. You deserve compassionate, individualized support that validates your concerns and takes into account your whole health.
Your Biology Wasn't Made for Today's Stress

Human metabolism was made to hold on to fat. When we're stressed, we crave sugar—a biology that is maladaptive for the chronic stresses of modern life. When women seek solutions, we know it's not because they did anything "wrong," but because of what's happened in their lives and in the world. Furthermore, it's normal for chronic stress and a history of trauma to increase insulin resistance and weight gain.
Your Care Should Connect the Dots

Metabolic challenges are present throughout life, but they become particularly critical during life transitions like menopause, where shifts in visceral fat lead to increased insulin resistance and cardiovascular risks. Conditions like metabolic syndrome, PCOS, diabetes, and hypertension shouldn’t be separated into the silos of endocrine, cardiac, and/or gynecologic care. Helping patients heal requires caring for those interactions within the context of the whole person and their life.

“When women come to Tia seeking weight support or metabolic health solutions, we know it’s not because they did anything “wrong,” but because of what’s happened in their lives (and in our world). It’s natural to need more support when your metabolism changes during menopause and other life transitions.”
Emily Lu, MD
, Medical Director Quality & Primary Care
How Tia is different for Weight & Metabolic Health

At Tia, we know that your weight and metabolism don't work in isolation—they are connected to your hormones, stress, and lifestyle. We don't focus only on the scale or offer a one-size-fits-all quick fix. Instead, we look beyond single solutions to understand how metabolic health connects with your mental, physical, and emotional well-being.
✓ Non-Judgmental Partnership

We approach weight and metabolism with compassionate, individualized support. We focus on your goals and utilize a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach, meaning your wellness is our priority, not fitting someone else's idea of a "healthy" body.
✓ Whole-Person Assessment

Our team takes time to listen, investigate root causes, and connect the dots across your health—including sleep, hormones, and mental health—to create a comprehensive care plan that reflects your goals and lifestyle.
✓ Expanded Toolkit & Evidence-Based Care

We offer options for both medication (including GLP-1s) and non-medication treatments, such as referrals to dedicated nutrition partners, medication management, and emotional support. Our care is evidence-based and personalized, not prescriptive.
✓ Long-Term Support

We're not here for a one-time prescription. Tia’s model is built for ongoing continuity of care through dedicated follow-up visits to track progress, adjust your plan, and ensure you feel supported over time.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

children.text: The Care Gap in Menopause
Menopause is a universal experience, yet most women are still left to navigate it alone. Up to
80% of women experience disruptive symptoms
such as hot flashes, sleep changes, mood shifts, or brain fog.
Despite how common this transition is, care for this life stage remains underresearched and underserved. Too often, women find themselves:
Told to "Wait it Out":
Being dismissed or told their symptoms are just "part of aging".
Misdiagnosed with Other Conditions:
Symptoms like anxiety or depression are treated without addressing the root hormonal cause.
Bounced Between Specialists:
Having to piece together disjointed care from multiple doctors who only treat one symptom at a time.
Uninformed:
In a
2023 U.S. survey
, only one in four women said they felt very knowledgeable about menopause, and two-thirds said more doctors trained in menopause care are needed.
This gap leaves millions of women struggling in silence, leading to years of preventable discomfort and missed opportunities to protect long-term health.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Where are you on your fertility journey?
The journey from fertility to postpartum can feel overwhelming. At Tia, we make sure you don’t have to navigate it alone.
Thinking About the Future

Whether you’re planning for the future, curious about your fertility, or actively trying to conceive, we’re here to help you understand your body and what influences it. At Tia, you’ll find a judgment-free space to talk through all the factors that can impact fertility, from hormones and cycle tracking to medications, supplements, and lifestyle. Our goal is to empower you with knowledge and personalized guidance.
Newly Pregnant

We provide primary care during your pregnancy and help with common symptoms like nausea. Our team can confirm your pregnancy and offer comprehensive all-options counseling so you feel empowered to make the best decision for your body and your life. We'll then connect you with an OB or midwife for prenatal care and delivery.
Postpartum and Beyond?

Having a baby brings big physical, mental, and emotional changes. It’s no wonder many new moms feel a sudden drop in care after birth. At Tia, we focus on long-term postpartum support that helps you recover, regain energy, and feel like yourself again. Our integrated care continues well after you’ve been cleared by your OB, helping you manage fatigue, anxiety, and the changes in your body.
Do These Symptoms Sound Familiar?
Fertility & Trying to Conceive
Struggling to understand your fertility, even if you aren't trying to conceive yet
Irregular or painful periods
Questions about why you aren't getting pregnant as quickly as you expected
Curious about fertility testing, cycle tracking, and fertility supplements
Postpartum & Beyond
You've been "cleared" by your OB, but you still don't feel like yourself
Fatigue, anxiety, mood swings, or a body that feels "off"
Pelvic pain, pain with sex, or changes in libido
Wondering if you're experiencing postpartum depression
Hair loss, changes in skin, or irregular periods after giving birth
Why care for Fertility & Postpartum Support?
Did you know?
Infertility is a disease of the male or female reproductive system defined by the failure to achieve a pregnancy after 12 months or more of regular unprotected sexual intercourse.
Estimates suggest that approximately one in every six people of reproductive age worldwide experience infertility in their lifetime.

In the male reproductive system, infertility is most commonly caused by problems in the ejection of semen (1), absence or low levels of sperm, or abnormal shape (morphology) and movement (motility) of the sperm.
In the female reproductive system, infertility may be caused by a range of abnormalities of the ovaries, uterus, fallopian tubes, and the endocrine system, among others.
Infertility can be primary or secondary. Primary infertility is when a pregnancy has never been achieved by a person, and secondary infertility is when at least one prior pregnancy has been achieved.

Fertility care encompasses the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility. Equal and equitable access to fertility care remains a challenge in most countries; particularly in low and middle-income countries.

"Your health journey should never be a lonely one. For too long, women have been pressured into fertility decisions or dismissed with a casual "you're fine" after childbirth when they know they're not. That stops here. We're building a new standard of care, one where you are heard, you are trusted, and you are treated as a whole person. This is a partnership, and you're in the driver's seat."
Courtney Domingo, NP
What is Tia's approach to your fertility journey?

At Tia, we believe your health is interconnected and your care should be, too. We don't just focus on a single body part. We zoom out to understand the whole picture of your health—physical, mental, and emotional, especially during major transitions of fertility through postpartum.
We Listen & Partner With You

We take the time to understand your full story and connect the dots between all aspects of your health. You’ll work with a team of experts trained in both modern and holistic modalities who listen deeply and tailor care to your needs. Together, we explore your cycle patterns, fertility goals, pregnancy history, postpartum recovery, and everything in between.
Evidence-Based Care

Our care is grounded in science, not trends or pressure. Whether it’s support for conception or recovery after birth, we focus on treatments and guidance proven to help you feel well and stay well. Every recommendation comes from a commitment to comprehensive, preventive care.
Long-Term Support

We’re not here for a one-time visit. Our model is designed for ongoing partnership as your health needs and goals evolve from preconception through pregnancy, postpartum, and beyond. We’ll be with you at every stage, helping you navigate changes and maintain long-term well-being.
Book an appointment
Every stage of fertility, pregnancy, and recovery deserves thoughtful, continuous care. Connect with Tia to get the support you need to feel balanced, confident, and well through it all.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.

What I want is to be proactive and be preventive... How do we make sure it doesn't get lost in all of the other things people think about when they hear longevity?”
Emily Lu, MD

Labs & Diagnostics

cells: Test
Lab / Panel Name
Description
Blood Sugar & Metabolism
Fasting Glucose, Insulin, C-peptide, Hemoglobin A1c, Insulin Resistance Panel
Examine how your body handles sugar—a big contributor to energy, weight, and long-term health.
Cholesterol & Heart Health
Lipid Panel (with reflex LDL), Apolipoprotein B, Lipoprotein(a), Lipoprotein Fractionation
We go beyond “good” and “bad” cholesterol to understand your real heart risk.
Inflammation & Stress
hs-CRP, Lp-PLA2 activity, Fibrinogen, IL‑6
Biomarkers for inflammation, which play a big role in aging and chronic disease.
Liver and kidney function
CBC, CMP, Urine microalbumin
Measure electrolyte balance and blood count and provide insight into how your metabolic organs (liver and kidney) are functioning.
Other Risk Signals
Homocysteine, Uric Acid, HIV, Syphilis
Identify your predisposition for stroke, heart disease, and hyperlipidemia later in life; certain infections also increase heart and dementia risk.
Brain & Cognitive Health
Vitamin D, Vitamin B12, Iron Studies, Omega‑3, Thyroid Panel, ApoE isoform testing
Nutrient deficiencies, thyroid issues, inflammation, and early dementia markers that can mimic or accelerate cognitive decline and brain aging.
Genetic Risk Panel
Comprehensive genetic risk panel (163 gene)
Screens for hereditary cancer, heart disease, metabolic diseases, and other medical conditions

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.

You’re unique. Your care should be too.

Questions about services?
1 Care Plan. 10 specialties. Fully connected.

Gynecology and sexual care, primary care, perimenopause & menopause, hormonal health, fertility & post partum support, weight & metabolic health, longevity, mental health, acupuncture, and skin care together under one roof—and in one coordinated Care Plan. No more bouncing between providers who never communicate. With Tia, every part of your health is treated as part of the whole, so you get care that actually works together—just like your body.
Gynecology and Sexual Health

From annual exams to navigating perimenopause and finding the right birth control, our expert providers take a holistic approach to care. We go beyond symptoms to see the bigger picture, offering ongoing support for concerns that need more than a one-time solution.
Our gynecological services include:

items: Well-woman exams (including pap tests & breast exams)
Contraception & family planning
Fertility care, from assessment to conception support
Perimenopause & menopause management
Abnormal bleeding
Vaginal, vulvar, or urinary symptoms (including STI testing & treatment)
PCOS, endometriosis, fibroids, & more

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Feeling low energy, mood swings, or sudden changes that do not feel like you? This new assessment helps connect the dots so you get clarity, a personalized plan, and the support to feel like yourself again.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.
Primary Care

We believe in preventing issues before they start so you can live as many healthy, vibrant years as possible. Our providers specialize in women’s health and partner with you to understand your unique needs—from preventative care to chronic condition management.
Our primary care services include:

items: Annual preventative exams & health screenings
Chronic condition management (e.g., diabetes, hypertension)
Abdominal & digestive issues
Sleep disturbances
Gender-affirming hormone therapy
Pain & headaches
Acute care for common illnesses & more

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.
Perimenopause & Menopause

Your hormones don’t just “wind down” during perimenopause and menopause, they shift in ways that can impact everything from mood and sleep to weight, libido, and how you feel in your body. Our clinicians take a whole person approach to these changes, looking at the interplay between hormones, lifestyle, and symptoms to understand what’s really going on. We then build a personalized Care Plan to help you manage symptoms, protect long term health, and navigate this transition feeling informed, supported, and in control.

items: Navigate hormonal shifts clinically and naturally
Conventional and integrative treatments like HRT, nutrition, supplements, lifestyle support and acupuncture
Feel more like yourself again

Your hormones are powerful messengers that influence everything from your mood and energy to digestion, skin, and sexual health. When they’re out of balance, the symptoms can feel confusing, disconnected, or hard to pinpoint. Our clinicians take a comprehensive look at your hormonal ecosystem, exploring patterns across your cycle, lifestyle, stress, sleep, and labs to uncover what’s really driving your symptoms. Then we create a personalized plan to rebalance hormones, ease discomfort, and help you feel more in tune with your body every day.

items: Get answers for irregular cycle, bloating & cramps, or PMS

Our clinicians look at the full picture of what is happening in your body, from hormones and insulin sensitivity to nutrition, stress, sleep, and lifestyle patterns. Longevity isn’t just about living longer, it’s about feeling better as you age. Your daily habits, hormones, metabolic health, and stress levels all influence how your body performs over time. Our clinicians look at the full picture, from labs and lifestyle to sleep, nutrition, and movement patterns, to understand what supports or accelerates aging in your unique system. Then we develop a personalized plan that helps you build strength, protect cognitive and metabolic health, and stay vibrant and resilient for the long haul.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director

children.text: The Problem

Feeling confused or judged about your experience with weight and metabolism is a common but frustrating experience. In fact, ~60% of patients report experiencing bias from doctors. When care focuses only on the scale or offers quick fixes, it often fails to look at the full picture of your physical, emotional, and metabolic health. With the rise of prescription solutions like GLP-1s, many women are also left wondering what is right for their body and how to navigate side effects or long-term management. This fragmented approach leaves you feeling stuck and like you've tried everything.

Why Weight & Metabolic Health matters for women
Metabolic Health is More Than a Number

Weight and metabolism are complex, often emotionally-charged subjects. Because metabolic health doesn't happen in a vacuum, our providers look at the bigger picture—from family history to hormones—to create a holistic path forward. We reject the idea that issues are caused by poor self-image or self-control. You deserve compassionate, individualized support that validates your concerns and takes into account your whole health.
Your Biology Wasn't Made for Today's Stress

Human metabolism was made to hold on to fat. When we're stressed, we crave sugar—a biology that is maladaptive for the chronic stresses of modern life. When women seek solutions, we know it's not because they did anything "wrong," but because of what's happened in their lives and in the world. Furthermore, it's normal for chronic stress and a history of trauma to increase insulin resistance and weight gain.
Your Care Should Connect the Dots

Metabolic challenges are present throughout life, but they become particularly critical during life transitions like menopause, where shifts in visceral fat lead to increased insulin resistance and cardiovascular risks. Conditions like metabolic syndrome, PCOS, diabetes, and hypertension shouldn’t be separated into the silos of endocrine, cardiac, and/or gynecologic care. Helping patients heal requires caring for those interactions within the context of the whole person and their life.

“When women come to Tia seeking weight support or metabolic health solutions, we know it’s not because they did anything “wrong,” but because of what’s happened in their lives (and in our world). It’s natural to need more support when your metabolism changes during menopause and other life transitions.”
Emily Lu, MD
, Medical Director Quality & Primary Care
How Tia is different for Weight & Metabolic Health

At Tia, we know that your weight and metabolism don't work in isolation—they are connected to your hormones, stress, and lifestyle. We don't focus only on the scale or offer a one-size-fits-all quick fix. Instead, we look beyond single solutions to understand how metabolic health connects with your mental, physical, and emotional well-being.
✓ Non-Judgmental Partnership

We approach weight and metabolism with compassionate, individualized support. We focus on your goals and utilize a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach, meaning your wellness is our priority, not fitting someone else's idea of a "healthy" body.
✓ Whole-Person Assessment

Our team takes time to listen, investigate root causes, and connect the dots across your health—including sleep, hormones, and mental health—to create a comprehensive care plan that reflects your goals and lifestyle.
✓ Expanded Toolkit & Evidence-Based Care

We offer options for both medication (including GLP-1s) and non-medication treatments, such as referrals to dedicated nutrition partners, medication management, and emotional support. Our care is evidence-based and personalized, not prescriptive.
✓ Long-Term Support

We're not here for a one-time prescription. Tia’s model is built for ongoing continuity of care through dedicated follow-up visits to track progress, adjust your plan, and ensure you feel supported over time.

is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.

children.text: The Care Gap in Menopause
Menopause is a universal experience, yet most women are still left to navigate it alone. Up to
80% of women experience disruptive symptoms
such as hot flashes, sleep changes, mood shifts, or brain fog.
Despite how common this transition is, care for this life stage remains underresearched and underserved. Too often, women find themselves:
Told to "Wait it Out":
Being dismissed or told their symptoms are just "part of aging".
Misdiagnosed with Other Conditions:
Symptoms like anxiety or depression are treated without addressing the root hormonal cause.
Bounced Between Specialists:
Having to piece together disjointed care from multiple doctors who only treat one symptom at a time.
Uninformed:
In a
2023 U.S. survey
, only one in four women said they felt very knowledgeable about menopause, and two-thirds said more doctors trained in menopause care are needed.
This gap leaves millions of women struggling in silence, leading to years of preventable discomfort and missed opportunities to protect long-term health.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted

Your care plan is tailored to you, not one-size-fits-all. We back your choices with evidence-based options, combining Menopausal Hormonal Therapy (MHT) with integrative interventions like acupuncture, nutrition, and mental health support from your care team.

You’re supported by a collaborative team of menopause-trained providers who stay with you through every phase of this journey. With continuous follow-ups and treatment management, your care remains connected, effective, and built for the long term.

children.text: Where are you on your fertility journey?
The journey from fertility to postpartum can feel overwhelming. At Tia, we make sure you don’t have to navigate it alone.
Thinking About the Future

Whether you’re planning for the future, curious about your fertility, or actively trying to conceive, we’re here to help you understand your body and what influences it. At Tia, you’ll find a judgment-free space to talk through all the factors that can impact fertility, from hormones and cycle tracking to medications, supplements, and lifestyle. Our goal is to empower you with knowledge and personalized guidance.
Newly Pregnant

We provide primary care during your pregnancy and help with common symptoms like nausea. Our team can confirm your pregnancy and offer comprehensive all-options counseling so you feel empowered to make the best decision for your body and your life. We'll then connect you with an OB or midwife for prenatal care and delivery.
Postpartum and Beyond?

Having a baby brings big physical, mental, and emotional changes. It’s no wonder many new moms feel a sudden drop in care after birth. At Tia, we focus on long-term postpartum support that helps you recover, regain energy, and feel like yourself again. Our integrated care continues well after you’ve been cleared by your OB, helping you manage fatigue, anxiety, and the changes in your body.
Do These Symptoms Sound Familiar?
Fertility & Trying to Conceive
Struggling to understand your fertility, even if you aren't trying to conceive yet
Irregular or painful periods
Questions about why you aren't getting pregnant as quickly as you expected
Curious about fertility testing, cycle tracking, and fertility supplements
Postpartum & Beyond
You've been "cleared" by your OB, but you still don't feel like yourself
Fatigue, anxiety, mood swings, or a body that feels "off"
Pelvic pain, pain with sex, or changes in libido
Wondering if you're experiencing postpartum depression
Hair loss, changes in skin, or irregular periods after giving birth
Why care for Fertility & Postpartum Support?
Did you know?
Infertility is a disease of the male or female reproductive system defined by the failure to achieve a pregnancy after 12 months or more of regular unprotected sexual intercourse.
Estimates suggest that approximately one in every six people of reproductive age worldwide experience infertility in their lifetime.

In the male reproductive system, infertility is most commonly caused by problems in the ejection of semen (1), absence or low levels of sperm, or abnormal shape (morphology) and movement (motility) of the sperm.
In the female reproductive system, infertility may be caused by a range of abnormalities of the ovaries, uterus, fallopian tubes, and the endocrine system, among others.
Infertility can be primary or secondary. Primary infertility is when a pregnancy has never been achieved by a person, and secondary infertility is when at least one prior pregnancy has been achieved.

Fertility care encompasses the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility. Equal and equitable access to fertility care remains a challenge in most countries; particularly in low and middle-income countries.

"Your health journey should never be a lonely one. For too long, women have been pressured into fertility decisions or dismissed with a casual "you're fine" after childbirth when they know they're not. That stops here. We're building a new standard of care, one where you are heard, you are trusted, and you are treated as a whole person. This is a partnership, and you're in the driver's seat."
Courtney Domingo, NP
What is Tia's approach to your fertility journey?

At Tia, we believe your health is interconnected and your care should be, too. We don't just focus on a single body part. We zoom out to understand the whole picture of your health—physical, mental, and emotional, especially during major transitions of fertility through postpartum.
We Listen & Partner With You

We take the time to understand your full story and connect the dots between all aspects of your health. You’ll work with a team of experts trained in both modern and holistic modalities who listen deeply and tailor care to your needs. Together, we explore your cycle patterns, fertility goals, pregnancy history, postpartum recovery, and everything in between.
Evidence-Based Care

Our care is grounded in science, not trends or pressure. Whether it’s support for conception or recovery after birth, we focus on treatments and guidance proven to help you feel well and stay well. Every recommendation comes from a commitment to comprehensive, preventive care.
Long-Term Support

We’re not here for a one-time visit. Our model is designed for ongoing partnership as your health needs and goals evolve from preconception through pregnancy, postpartum, and beyond. We’ll be with you at every stage, helping you navigate changes and maintain long-term well-being.
Book an appointment
Every stage of fertility, pregnancy, and recovery deserves thoughtful, continuous care. Connect with Tia to get the support you need to feel balanced, confident, and well through it all.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.
Why our members love Tia

Why care for Longevity matters

“With longevity, we specifically mean expanding your health span, so, expanding the amount of time that you live to your greatest vitality... and that's actually the key to helping people live longer. What I want is to be proactive and be preventive... How do we make sure it doesn't get lost in all of the other things people think about when they hear longevity?”
Emily Lu, MD

Labs & Diagnostics

cells: Test
Lab / Panel Name
Description
Blood Sugar & Metabolism
Fasting Glucose, Insulin, C-peptide, Hemoglobin A1c, Insulin Resistance Panel
Examine how your body handles sugar—a big contributor to energy, weight, and long-term health.
Cholesterol & Heart Health
Lipid Panel (with reflex LDL), Apolipoprotein B, Lipoprotein(a), Lipoprotein Fractionation
We go beyond “good” and “bad” cholesterol to understand your real heart risk.
Inflammation & Stress
hs-CRP, Lp-PLA2 activity, Fibrinogen, IL‑6
Biomarkers for inflammation, which play a big role in aging and chronic disease.
Liver and kidney function
CBC, CMP, Urine microalbumin
Measure electrolyte balance and blood count and provide insight into how your metabolic organs (liver and kidney) are functioning.
Other Risk Signals
Homocysteine, Uric Acid, HIV, Syphilis
Identify your predisposition for stroke, heart disease, and hyperlipidemia later in life; certain infections also increase heart and dementia risk.
Brain & Cognitive Health
Vitamin D, Vitamin B12, Iron Studies, Omega‑3, Thyroid Panel, ApoE isoform testing
Nutrient deficiencies, thyroid issues, inflammation, and early dementia markers that can mimic or accelerate cognitive decline and brain aging.
Genetic Risk Panel
Comprehensive genetic risk panel (163 gene)
Screens for hereditary cancer, heart disease, metabolic diseases, and other medical conditions

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted

faqs.question: What services are included the Tia paid membership?

children.text: You have access to all of Tia’s appointment types, delivered by expert women's health providers. We will bill medical services to your insurance (if you are in-network), and you will be responsible for co-pay, coinsurance, or deductibles

faqs.question: What services are included in Tia Essential?

children.text: Yes! Tia accepts most major PPOs for primary care and gynecological services (co-pays and deductible costs still apply). Coverage varies by clinic location and your insurance coverage for acupuncture and mental health services. Depending on your membership plan, we will give you a step-by-step guide on how to assess your network status for those services, or your care team will check for you.
At Tia, we’ve reimagined the doctor’s office to feel like a space designed
for you

. Warm, welcoming, and thoughtfully designed, our offices are built to put you at ease the moment you walk in. From primary care and gynecology to acupuncture and beyond, we offer comprehensive services under one roof—delivered by experts who understand the full picture of women’s health. You won’t need to go to one place for your PCP visit and another for your GYN. It’s all here—all at Tia.
